# Cai

> Cai is a free, open-source macOS menu bar app that runs AI actions on your clipboard.

## What is Cai?

Cai is a clipboard AI assistant for Mac. Select any text or image, press ⌥C, and run smart actions — summarize, translate, OCR, create GitHub issues, Linear tickets, and more. It runs 100% locally with a built-in AI model (Ministral 3B), supports Apple Intelligence on macOS 26+, and works with external providers like LM Studio and Ollama. No cloud required.

## Key Features

- Smart context-aware actions on clipboard content
- Built-in AI model (Ministral 3B) — works offline, no setup needed
- Apple Intelligence support (macOS 26+, M1 or later)
- Create GitHub issues and Linear tickets from any app
- Image to text (OCR) from screenshots and images
- Custom prompts, shell commands, and URL actions
- Route results to Slack, Notion, Mail, or any app via custom destinations
- Clipboard history with search and pin
- Works with LM Studio, Ollama, Jan AI, and cloud providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Mistral)
- Community extensions — browse and install actions shared by others
- Keyboard-first design — type to filter, ⌘1–9 to execute
- Free and open source forever (MIT license)

## How It Works

1. Copy — Highlight text or an image in any app
2. Summon — Press ⌥C to open the action menu
3. Act — Pick an action (summarize, translate, create ticket, run custom prompt, etc.)

## Links

- Homepage: https://getcai.app
- Documentation: https://getcai.app/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/clipboard-ai/cai
- Extensions: https://github.com/clipboard-ai/cai-extensions
- Release Notes: https://github.com/clipboard-ai/cai/releases
